name: Ensure weak TLS Protocols are not used for ELB
body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when an internet-facing AWS load balancer listener uses an SSL policy that permits old TLS versions such as TLS 1.0 or TLS 1.1. The query checks the listener SSL policy on internet-facing ELBs.

Use a modern ELB security policy that only allows TLS 1.2 or newer.
description: Internet-facing AWS load balancers using SSL policies that allow weak TLS protocols.
timedControlSummary: AWS load balancers should be moved away from weak TLS policies before legacy-client compatibility becomes a blocker.
timedControlDisruptionRisk: Changing listener policies can break legacy clients if compatibility is not tested ahead of time.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Update each affected HTTPS/TLS listener to a modern SSL policy.

1. Open **EC2 > Load Balancers**.
2. Select the affected internet-facing load balancer.
3. Open **Listeners** and choose each HTTPS or TLS listener.
4. Change the security policy to a modern TLS 1.2+ policy, such as `ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06` or the current AWS recommended policy for your load balancer type.
5. Save the listener configuration and test client compatibility.

CLI example for ALB/NLB listeners:

```bash
aws elbv2 modify-listener --listener-arn <listener-arn> --ssl-policy ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 --region {{asset.region}}
```

Cyscale will pass when no internet-facing listener uses one of the weak SSL policies listed in the control query.
